refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly state the latency metric and end-to-end scope, clarifying the performance requirement critical for safety.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of the teleoperation communication path under defined network conditions using telemetry data and network monitoring tools.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link quality to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to monitor latency performance.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize data encoding and compression algorithms to reduce payload size.
DP2: Use dedicated low-latency network protocols and QoS prioritization.
DP3: Implement edge computing nodes to process commands closer to the vehicle.
DP4: Employ telemetry buffering and predictive algorithms to mask latency spikes.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP2: Use dedicated low-latency network protocols and QoS prioritization.

potential_sources: Dependency on network infrastructure and protocols
Interaction with telemetry and tasking interfaces
coupling_check.notes: DP2 logically isolates latency control to network protocol and QoS layers, minimizing coupling with vehicle control logic.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports QoS and low-latency protocols.
Risk of network congestion or failure impacting latency despite protocol optimization.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding fallback mechanisms or redundancy in network protocols to mitigate risks.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing teleoperation communication, including network protocol handling and latency optimization.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection used for teleoperation data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send latency and network performance metrics to monitoring systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands from higher-level control modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of latency-related DP; low diffusion as latency logic is centralized.
Vehicle module depends on teleoperation data but does not control latency optimization.
Safety Services monitors alerts but does not implement latency control.
Overall system module with broad scope; less focused containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency control DP is best contained within the Teleoperation module to centralize responsibility and minimize diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on network infrastructure outside module control.
Risk of interface overload if telemetry data volume is high.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and monitor telemetry data rates to avoid overload.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (network protocol and QoS prioritization) directly addresses the refined FR of minimizing latency with clear assumptions and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as latency logic is centralized in Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best ownership candidate for the DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified via telemetry interface and network monitoring tools within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency control DP to Teleoperation IM with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add fallback or redundancy strategies in DP to mitigate network risks.
Clarify telemetry data rate limits to prevent interface overload.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, risks related to network infrastructure and telemetry data volume require refinement in DP and IM definitions.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link quality to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to monitor latency performance.

revise.why: Incorporate fallback mechanisms and redundancy to address network risks and improve robustness.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports QoS and low-latency protocols.
Telemetry data volume can be managed within interface capacity.
open_risks: Network congestion or failure impacting latency.
Telemetry interface overload due to high data volume.
evidence_needed: Network performance tests under expected conditions.
Telemetry data rate measurements and interface stress tests.
